_comment: Locality-hint demo: an ASYMMETRIC join used to evaluate the `split` cut hint.
Two pinned producers feed one auto-placed `join`; the partitioner must cut one
of the two producer→join edges to place `join`.  Each producer declares the
breakability of its OWN output edge:
heavy: split=avoid  → don't cut here (keep the consumer local)
light: split=prefer → safe to cut here
So `join` colocates with `heavy` (host 0) and the cross-machine cut lands on the
LIGHT edge.  check_locality.py flips the hints and asserts the cut follows.
Partitioned with EMPTY hints so every host has quota and dep-affinity (the hint)
decides placement (Pack/balanced policies would force the single auto node onto
one host before the hint could act).
